senses.definition: a type of extinct reef-building coral that lived during the Mesozoic era
examples: Fossils of rudists are found in limestone from the Cretaceous period.
The era's reefs were dominated by rudists rather than modern corals.

senses.definition: Spanish feminine form of 'rudist' or a name; may refer to a person, place, or title in some contexts
examples: La rudista es un término técnico; en contextos educativos, se explica la criatura marina.
Rudista could be a surname in Spanish-speaking countries.

senses.definition: plural form related to rudist (biological term); used in scientific naming for multiple rudists
examples: Fossils labeled rudistae indicate a diverse rudist community.
The rudistae assemblage shows a wide range of reef-building species.

senses.definition: an extinct coral or a fossilized reef-forming organism belonging to the Rudistida clade
examples: Geologists studied rudistan fossils to understand ancient reefs.
Rudistan shells are often found in limestone formations.

senses.definition: a member of the order Rudistida, an extinct group of reef-building organisms
examples: Rudistids dominated ancient seas during the Cretaceous.
Fossils of rudistids reveal information about past oceans.

senses.definition: plural of rudistid; multiple extinct reef-building organisms
examples: The rudistids fossils were abundant in that formation.
Rudistids indicate a reef system different from modern corals.

senses.definition: plural of rudist; reef-building marine organisms from the past
examples: Rudists were common in the Cretaceous seas.
Studying rudists helps scientists understand ancient marine environments.

senses.definition: the quality or condition of being rude or coarse; lack of refinement
examples: The rude comment showed a lack of rudity in his upbringing.
She disliked the rudity of their jokes.

senses.definition: a surname or proper noun, possibly of German origin
examples: Rudloff is a last name found in some German-speaking communities.
The Rudloff family has historical records in the region.

senses.definition: a variant or misspelling of 'Christmas' or a non-standard term; likely not a standard word
examples: Rudmasday appears in some nonstandard spellings of a December holiday.

senses.definition: a male given name; also a well-known historical figure (e.g., Rudolf Steiner, Rudolf Nureyev)
examples: Rudolf is his given name.
Rudolf Steiner contributed to anthroposophy.

senses.definition: a male given name; also the well-known red-nosed reindeer from Christmas folklore
examples: Rudolph is a common name in many countries.
Rudolph the Red-Nosed Reindeer is a popular Christmas character.

senses.definition: relating to or named after Rudolph; sometimes used to describe a style or person associated with Rudolf or Rudolph
examples: Rudolphine features appeared in the painting style.
A rudolphine influence can be seen in the sculpture.

senses.definition: a masculine given name, Latinized form used in historical or scholarly contexts
examples: Rudolphus was referenced in classical texts.
The scholar used Rudolphus as the Latin form of Rudolf.

senses.definition: archaic or rare form of 'rude'; lacking refinement or civility
examples: A rudous remark would offend many listeners.

senses.definition: short for 'rudiments' or a casual plural referring to unrefined things; could be slang for 'roughs' or 'RUDs' as an acronym in specific contexts
examples: He kept the basics in his notes, the ruds he could improve later.

senses.definition: a nickname or given name; also used to describe someone who is Rudy; can imply craftiness in informal use
examples: Rudy is my friend's name.
A Rudy plan sometimes feels clever but risky.

senses.definition: to feel sorry or regret something
examples: I rue the decision I made yesterday.
She will rue ignoring the warning.

senses.definition: a narrow street or alley, especially in a European city
examples: We wandered down a quiet rue in Paris.
The café sits on a cobbled rue near the cathedral.

senses.definition: past tense of rue; felt regret or sorrow about something
examples: He rued his hasty words after the argument.
She rued missing the bus and arriving late.

senses.definition: Spanish for 'wheel' or a type of belt drive; may appear in loanword usage
examples: La rueda rota necesita reparación.
El sistema utiliza una rueda de engranaje llamada rueda.

senses.definition: Spanish plural of rueda; wheels
examples: Las ruedas están allando la bicicleta.
Cambiaron las ruedas del coche.

senses.definition: feeling or showing regret or sorrow; mournful or pensive
examples: She gave a rueful smile after the awkward joke.
There was a rueful tone in his voice as he apologized.

senses.definition: in a way that shows regret or sadness
examples: He shook his head ruefully.
She ruefully admitted the mistake.

senses.definition: the quality of feeling regret or sorrow; a rueful attitude
examples: There was a note of ruefulness in his apology.
Her ruefulness softened the harsh critique.

senses.definition: plural of ruefulness; multiple instances of regretful feeling
examples: The collection displayed various ruefulnesses across the letters.
